data.title: Solana-based True platform aims to provide smarter, transparent execution in decentralized markets through AI; targets U.S. market.
data.description: Benjamin Bilski, representing the Solana-based AI trading platform True, mentioned that the accessibility of perpetual futures in the U.S. could validate their model and grow the market. The comment highlights the potential impact of regulatory clarity on decentralized derivatives and AI-driven trading. Bilski stated that although they do not actively target the U.S. market, global regulatory clarity is welcomed. He emphasized that if perpetuals are approved in the U.S., the model would be validated and market size expanded. He also noted that innovation in decentralized finance will persist with users still seeking transparency, instant access, and self-custody. True is set to launch its $TRUE token sale to support the first AI-native perpetuals DEX on Solana, aiming to combine high-speed off-chain execution.

data.title: Celestia (TIA) stabilizes around $1.61, up 1.71%, showing mixed outlook with neutral momentum despite bearish trend pressure.
data.description: The current price of TIA stands steady at $1.61, showing a daily increase of 1.71%. A technical analysis suggests a neutral momentum, but there is bearish pressure from moving averages indicating a downward trend in the medium-term.

data.title: New Era Energy & Digital, Inc. (Nasdaq: NUAI) faces delisting for not meeting $50 million market value rule.
data.description: New Era Energy & Digital, Inc. (Nasdaq: NUAI) based in MIDLAND, Texas, revealed that on September 3, 2025, the company was informed by the Staff that it had not met the MVLS Rule as of September 2, 2025. This rule mandates maintaining a market value of listed securities of a minimum of $50 million. In failing to comply, the company faces delisting unless a hearing is promptly requested.

data.title: Children's Place Q2 2025: Net sales down 6.8% to $298.0M, operating income $4.1M, $78M inventory reduction, plan to save $40M+ in next 3 years, includes transformation initiative, $20M-$25M tariff projection, 494 stores open. (Character count: 269)
data.description: The Children's Place, Inc. reported its financial results for the second fiscal quarter ended August 2, 2025. Net sales decreased by 6.8% to $298.0 million compared to $319.7 million the previous year. Gross profit decreased to $101.3 million, while operating income improved to $4.1 million compared to an operating loss of $21.8 million the previous year. The Company plans to implement transformation initiatives to streamline operations and yield over $40 million in gross benefits over the next three years. Additionally, they are projecting approximately $20-25 million in additional tariff and duty expenses for fiscal year 2025 but are working on mitigating 80% of these impacts. The second quarter saw a net loss of $5.4 million compared to a net loss of $32.1 million in the previous year. Finally, the Company ended the quarter with 494 stores. More details and non-GAAP financial information are available in the full press release.

data.title: MasTec CFO to speak at Morgan Stanley Investor Conference on Sept 10th at 3:20 pm PT. Event accessible on the company's website.
data.description: MasTec, Inc. revealed that Paul DiMarco, its Executive VP and CFO, will join a fireside chat at the Morgan Stanley 13th Annual Laguna Conference on September 10th at around 3:20 p.m. Pacific Time. The webcast will be available on MasTec's website, www.mastec.com, under the Investors tab. For any schedule adjustments, interested individuals are encouraged to monitor the website. This update came from a recent announcement by the company, listed on NYSE as MTZ.

data.title: Lennox's commercial HVAC unit is first to pass DOE's validation in 15-25 tons category. DALLAS, Sept. 5, 2025.
data.description: Lennox Commercial HVAC has achieved a significant milestone by having its cold climate heat pump rooftop unit become the inaugural product in the 15-25 tons category of the Commercial Building HVAC Technology Challenge to pass lab validation. The validation was announced in Dallas on September 5, 2025. Lennox, identified by the NYSE as LII and recognized for its energy-efficient climate solutions, has made a remarkable entry in this sector.

data.title: Sabra Health Care REIT, Inc. executives to attend 2025 NIC Fall Conference on Sept 8-10 in Austin, Texas. #SBRA
data.description: Sabra Health Care REIT, Inc. revealed that key executives including Darrin Smith, the Executive Vice President of Investments, Eliza Gozar, the Senior Vice President of Investments, and Kara Pappanduros, the Senior Vice President of Asset Management, will be present at the 2025 NIC Fall Conference from September 8 to 10, 2025, at the JW Marriott Austin in Austin, Texas. This event will also include other members of the company. This announcement was made by the company, listed on Nasdaq as SBRA.

data.title: Powerball jackpot nears $1.8 billion, prompting dreams of new homes and secret triumph for ticket buyers.
data.description: Ahead of Saturday's drawing, the Powerball jackpot reached a staggering $1.8 billion, marking it as the second-largest jackpot in history. This massive prize has sparked excitement among countless individuals, inspiring them to participate in the upcoming drawing.
